workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Alpine Ski House escalation email. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 14:19 EST
Subject: partner engagement - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 10:13 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with partner engagement. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-I trap (Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-I failure mode (Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-I -- Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant
